problems.title: 1-D Array Creation
problems.description: Create a 1-D array with elements [1, 2, 3], and display its dtype and ndim. Expected output: dtype: int32, ndim: 1. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a 1-D array with elements [1, 2, 3], and display its dtype and ndim. Expected output: dtype: int32, ndim: 1
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(arr):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr = array([1, 2, 3])
    main(arr)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(arr):
    print(arr.dtype)
    print(arr.ndim)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr = array([1, 2, 3])
    main(arr)

problems.title: 2-D Array Creation
problems.description: Create a 2-D array with elements [[1, 2], [3, 4]], and display its shape and size. Expected output: shape: (2, 2), size: 4. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a 2-D array with elements [[1, 2], [3, 4]], and display its shape and size. Expected output: shape: (2, 2), size: 4
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(arr2d):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr2d = array([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
    main(arr2d)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(arr2d):
    print(arr2d.shape)
    print(arr2d.size)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr2d = array([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
    main(arr2d)

problems.title: Array Reshaping 1-D to 2-D
problems.description: Reshape the 1-D array [1, 2, 3, 4] to a 2-D array of shape (2, 2). Expected output: [[1 2] [3 4]]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Reshape the 1-D array [1, 2, 3, 4] to a 2-D array of shape (2, 2). Expected output: [[1 2] [3 4]]
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1, input_2, input_3):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3, 4]

    input_2 = 2

    input_3 = 2
    main(input_1, input_2, input_3)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1, input_2, input_3):
    arr_reshape = array(input_1).reshape(input_2, input_3)
    print(arr_reshape)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3, 4]

    input_2 = 2

    input_3 = 2
    main(input_1, input_2, input_3)

problems.title: Array Reshaping 1-D to 3-D
problems.description: Reshape the 1-D array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8] to a 3-D array of shape (2, 2, 2). Expected output: [[[1 2] [3 4]] [[5 6] [7 8]]]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Reshape the 1-D array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8] to a 3-D array of shape (2, 2, 2). Expected output: [[[1 2] [3 4]] [[5 6] [7 8]]]
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1, input_2, input_3, input_4):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]

    input_2 = 2

    input_3 = 2

    input_4 = 2
    main(input_1, input_2, input_3, input_4)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1, input_2, input_3, input_4):
    arr3d = array(input_1).reshape(input_2, input_3, input_4)
    print(arr3d)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]

    input_2 = 2

    input_3 = 2

    input_4 = 2
    main(input_1, input_2, input_3, input_4)

problems.title: Matrix Creation from Array
problems.description: Convert the 2-D array [[1, 2], [3, 4]] to a matrix. Expected output: matrix([[1, 2], [3, 4]]). Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Convert the 2-D array [[1, 2], [3, 4]] to a matrix. Expected output: matrix([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [[1, 2], [3, 4]]
    main(input_1)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1):
    matrix_from_arr = matrix(input_1)
    print(matrix_from_arr)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [[1, 2], [3, 4]]
    main(input_1)

problems.title: Matrix Diagonal
problems.description: Print the diagonal elements of the matrix [[1, 2], [3, 4]]. Expected output: [1 4]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Print the diagonal elements of the matrix [[1, 2], [3, 4]]. Expected output: [1 4]
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(matrix_diag):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    matrix_diag = matrix('1 2; 3 4')
    main(matrix_diag)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(matrix_diag):
    matrix_diag.diagonal()
    print(matrix_diag.diagonal())

if __name__ == "__main__":
    matrix_diag = matrix('1 2; 3 4')
    main(matrix_diag)

problems.title: Direct Matrix Creation
problems.description: Create a matrix directly using the 'matrix()' attribute with the input '1 2 3 6 ; 4 5 6 7'. Expected output: matrix([[1, 2, 3, 6], [4, 5, 6, 7]]). Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a matrix directly using the 'matrix()' attribute with the input '1 2 3 6 ; 4 5 6 7'. Expected output: matrix([[1, 2, 3, 6], [4, 5, 6, 7]])
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = '1 2 3 6 ; 4 5 6 7'
    main(input_1)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1):
    mat = matrix(input_1)
    print(mat)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = '1 2 3 6 ; 4 5 6 7'
    main(input_1)
